findings.message: Hardcoded secret committed to source: `api_secret_key` defaults to the literal string "super-secret-key-do-not-share". Committed secrets are exposed to anyone with repo access (including git history forever), can be scraped by automated secret scanners, and become the production value whenever the `APP_API_SECRET_KEY` env var is not set — a classic OWASP A02 cryptographic failure / A05 security misconfiguration. Any signing/encryption/auth that relies on this key is effectively unkeyed.
findings.suggestion: Remove the default entirely and require the value from the environment: `api_secret_key: SecretStr` (no default) so Pydantic raises at startup if `APP_API_SECRET_KEY` is unset. Use `pydantic.SecretStr` to prevent accidental logging. Rotate the leaked value immediately and purge it from git history (e.g. git filter-repo).
